title: Class 3 Digital Signature Certificate
description: Class 3 DSC for e-tendering, e-procurement, high-security signing. Same-day issuance. ₹2,500. Virtual Auditor.
bodyText:
Class 3 DSC provides the highest level of assurance. Required for: e-tendering on government portals (GEM, IREPS, CPPP), e-procurement, high-value contract signing, and applications requiring enhanced identity verification. Virtual Auditor provides Class 3 DSC with same-day issuance. Quick Answer: Class 3 Digital Signature Certificate — Class 3 DSC for e-tendering, e-procurement, high-security signing. Same-day issuance. ₹2,500. Virtual Auditor.

What is Class 3 Digital Signature Certificate?

Class 3 Digital Signature Certificate is a service offered by Virtual Auditor, an AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm (IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333) led by CA V. Viswanathan (FCA, ACS, CFE, IBBI RV), specialising in company registration under the Companies Act, 2013, from offices in Chennai, Bangalore, and Mumbai since 2012.
Source: Companies Act 2013, Companies (Incorporation) Rules 2014, MCA Circulars Official References: MCA Filing Portal ↗ · SPICe+ Form ↗
HEADING2: Regulatory Framework
Class 3 Digital Signature Certificates are governed by the Information Technology Act, 2000 and IT (Certifying Authorities) Rules. Class 3 DSC provides the highest level of assurance and is required for e-tendering, e-procurement portals (GeM, IREPS, CPPP), and high-value transactions. Requires in-person verification by the Certifying Authority.
HEADING3: Class 3 DSC — Use Cases

Platform | Purpose | Class 3 Required |
GeM | Government e-Marketplace | Yes (mandatory) |
IREPS | Indian Railways procurement | Yes |
State e-Tender | State government tenders | Yes |
CPPP | Central Public Procurement | Yes |
HEADING2: People Also Ask

HEADING3: What documents are needed for company registration in India?

PAN Card, Aadhaar, passport-size photo, address proof, registered office proof (rent agreement + NOC or property document), and utility bill. For foreign directors: apostilled passport and address proof. Virtual Auditor provides a detailed checklist at engagement.

HEADING3: How long does company registration take in India?

5-15 working days depending on MCA processing time and name availability. SPICe+ integrates name reservation, incorporation, PAN/TAN, and GST in one application.

HEADING2: Class 3 Digital Signature Certificate — Enhanced Security

Class 3 DSC provides the highest level of identity assurance. It is mandatory for government e-procurement, e-tendering, and high-security transactions where Class 2 is not accepted.

HEADING3: Class 2 vs Class 3 Comparison

Feature | Class 2 DSC | Class 3 DSC |

Identity Verification | Video KYC (remote) | In-person verification (mandatory) |

Security Level | Medium | Highest |

Cost | ₹800-₹1,500 | ₹2,000-₹3,500 |

MCA Filing | Accepted | Accepted (overkill) |

e-Tendering | Not accepted | Mandatory |

Processing Time | 1-3 days | 2-5 days |

Where Class 3 is mandatory: GeM (Government e-Marketplace) for procurement above ₹25,000. IREPS (Indian Railways Electronic Procurement System). Central Public Procurement Portal (CPP). Various state e-procurement portals. Defence procurement portals.

In-person verification: Unlike Class 2, Class 3 requires the applicant to appear before the Certifying Authority (or authorised agent) with original documents. Some CAs offer doorstep verification service for an additional fee. This higher verification standard is what makes Class 3 more secure and suitable for high-value transactions.

Source: IT Act, 2000. CCA Guidelines on DSC. CCA India ↗

HEADING3: ⚡ How Virtual Auditor Delivers This Differently

Our compliance calendar tracks every post-registration deadline: auditor appointment (30 days), INC-20A (180 days), board meetings (quarterly), AGM (6 months from year-end), AOC-4 and MGT-7 (annual). Proactive reminders prevent penalties. Same team handles registration through first annual filing and beyond.

HEADING3: Need Help With This?

Free 30-minute consultation with CA V. Viswanathan, FCA, ACS, CFE, IBBI RV. No obligation.

HEADING3: What You Will Receive
Upon completion, you will receive: Class 3 Digital Signature Certificate with encryption on USB token, portal registration across MCA/TRACES/e-filing/GST portals, driver installation and testing on your computer, user guide for DSC usage and troubleshooting, and renewal reminder setup. Class 3 DSC is mandatory for company directors, authorised signatories, and professionals filing statutory returns. Validity: 2 years.
HEADING3: Latest Regulatory Updates (FY 2025-26)
This page has been updated to reflect changes introduced in Budget 2025, recent notifications from CBDT, CBIC, MCA, SEBI, and RBI, and evolving compliance requirements for FY 2025-26. Virtual Auditor continuously monitors regulatory developments to ensure all advice and filings are current and compliant with the latest provisions.
HEADING2: Recent Engagement — How We Helped
Context: a growing e-commerce startup that needed to transition from a sole proprietorship to a private limited company to raise angel funding.
Challenge: The business had existing GST registration, bank accounts, vendor contracts, and marketplace seller accounts all under the proprietorship. A smooth transition was needed without disrupting operations or losing marketplace seller ratings.
Our approach: We structured the transition as a business transfer under a slump sale arrangement, incorporated the new Pvt Ltd company, obtained fresh GST registration, and coordinated the transfer of all marketplace accounts. We handled FSSAI license transfer, updated all vendor agreements, and ensured GST continuity through proper input credit transfer under Section 18(1)(d).
Outcome: The entire transition was completed in 18 working days with zero disruption to daily operations. The angel round of Rs 75 lakhs closed within 6 weeks of incorporation. The company is now using our ongoing compliance service for annual filings, GST returns, and statutory audit.
This engagement illustrates Virtual Auditor's approach to class 3 digital signature certificate — combining regulatory expertise with practical execution to deliver results within the client's timeline.
HEADING2: When Is Class 3 Digital Signature Certificate Not Required?
A Class 3 DSC may not be required when: (a) you are not a company director, authorised signatory, or professional required to file statutory returns, (b) an existing valid DSC covers all your filing requirements, (c) the entity is a sole proprietorship with no digital filing obligations, or (d) you have delegated all filing responsibilities to a professional. Class 3 DSC is mandatory for MCA filings, GST return signing (for companies), and income tax return signing for entities requiring audit.
If you are unsure whether your situation requires class 3 digital signature certificate, contact us for a free preliminary assessment. We will advise you honestly — including telling you if you do not need our services.

faqs.q: When is Class 3 needed instead of Class 2?
faqs.a: For government e-tendering portals (GeM, CPPP, IREPS) and e-procurement. If you bid on government contracts, you need Class 3.

faqs.q: What is Class 3 DSC used for?
faqs.a: Government e-tendering (GeM, IREPS, CPP Portal), high-security e-procurement, and applications requiring enhanced identity assurance.

faqs.q: How is Class 3 different from Class 2?
faqs.a: Class 3 requires mandatory in-person verification (not video KYC). Highest assurance level. Cost: Rs 2,000-3,500. Processing: 2-5 days.
GeM (Government e-Marketplace), IREPS (Indian Railways), CPP Portal, various state e-procurement portals, and defence procurement.

faqs.q: Can I upgrade Class 2 to Class 3?
faqs.a: No direct upgrade. Must purchase separate Class 3 DSC. Both can coexist for same person on different tokens.

faqs.q: Is Class 3 needed for company registration?
faqs.a: No. Class 2 sufficient for MCA, IT, GST. Class 3 only for e-tendering and specific high-security government portals.
